demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility; normally mobilises with a frame

contact_nok.relationship: aged care facility nursing staff (present and initiating the call on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This is the nurse calling from the aged care home about one of our residents. Since yesterday she's gone very weak and tired, she's dizzy every time she tries to stand, and she nearly fainted moving from the bed to the chair this morning. She told me her chest feels heavy. This afternoon she seemed a bit confused for a while. When we checked, her pulse was only in the 40s and her blood pressure looked low, so we wanted advice.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden decline over the last day in a frail resident — very weak, dizzy on standing, a near-faint on transfer, and a pulse the staff measured in the 40s — the staff want urgent advice.

symptom_narrative.onset: profound weakness and lightheadedness came on and worsened over about the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly a day, acutely worse today
symptom_narrative.character: very weak and lethargic, lightheaded and unsteady, with a heavy feeling in the chest; nearly fainted when moving from bed to chair
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the staff say she is now too dizzy to get up and is staying in bed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole-body weakness and dizziness; heaviness across the chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse through the day, not just when she stands
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help; lying down doesn't fully settle it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to sit up or stand makes the dizziness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: brief episode of confusion this afternoon (noted by carers); no true blackout; no sweating spells reported
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the staff say she has been getting more tired and less able to get about for several weeks, but nothing this severe before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks with a frame; today she is effectively bedbound because of the dizziness and fall risk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a heart condition that she takes heart tablets for
past_medical_history.how_managed: long-term medication managed through the facility

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility with nursing staff on site; normally mobilises with a frame; carers present and assisting

objective_data_offered.timing: checked by facility staff just before the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: manual/monitor pulse count by facility nursing staff, not a diagnostic ECG; rate and rhythm not confirmed
single facility blood-pressure reading; cuff calibration unknown

objective_data_offered.value: staff say she looks pale, is lethargic and slow to respond but knows who and where she is
